lesson.title: Combining Like Terms
lesson.body: **Like terms** have the same variable(s) raised to the same power(s). Only like terms can be added or subtracted — you combine their **coefficients** and keep the variable part unchanged. Examples: 3x + 7x = 10x; 5x² − 2x² = 3x². Unlike terms (like 4x and 4x²) cannot be combined. When simplifying, also apply the distributive property first to clear parentheses, then collect like terms.

rooms.question: Simplify: 7x + 3x
rooms.explanation: 7x + 3x = (7+3)x = 10x. Think of x as a unit: 7 'x's plus 3 'x's = 10 'x's. The 10x² error wrongly multiplies the variables. The 21x error multiplies coefficients instead of adding them.

rooms.question: Simplify: 5x² + 3x − 2x² + 4x
rooms.explanation: Collect x² terms: 5x² − 2x² = 3x². Collect x terms: 3x + 4x = 7x. Result: 3x² + 7x. The terms cannot be further combined because x² and x are unlike terms.

rooms.question: Simplify: 2(3x − 4) + 5x
rooms.explanation: Distribute 2: 2(3x) − 2(4) = 6x − 8. Then add 5x: 6x + 5x − 8 = 11x − 8. The 11x+8 error forgets the negative sign during distribution. The 16x error drops the constant −8.

rooms.question: Simplify: 4(x + 2) − 3(2x − 1)
rooms.explanation: Distribute: 4x + 8 − 6x + 3. Note: −3 × (−1) = +3. Collect x terms: 4x − 6x = −2x. Constants: 8 + 3 = 11. Result: −2x + 11. The −2x + 5 error computes −3 × (−1) = −3 (missing the sign flip).

rooms.question: Identify which terms are like terms with '3x²y':
items: 3xy² — NO (y has power 2, x has power 1)
rooms.explanation: Like terms must match on every variable and every exponent: 3x²y and −5x²y both have x² and y¹, so they are like terms. 3xy² has x¹y², and 7x²y² has x²y² — neither matches x²y¹. Coefficients are irrelevant to whether terms are 'like.'

boss.name: The Term Collector
boss.taunt: Only like terms survive. Are you like anything?

questions.question: Simplify: 3(2x − 5) − (x + 4) + 2x
questions.explanation: Distribute: 6x − 15 − x − 4 + 2x. Note −(x+4) = −x − 4 (multiply each term by −1). Collect x: 6x − x + 2x = 7x. Constants: −15 − 4 = −19. Result: 7x − 19. The 5x error loses the +2x term.

questions.question: The perimeter of a rectangle is 2(3x + 1) + 2(x − 2). Simplified, the perimeter is:
questions.explanation: Distribute: 6x + 2 + 2x − 4. Collect: 6x + 2x = 8x; 2 − 4 = −2. P = 8x − 2. For this to represent a real rectangle, x must be large enough that both dimensions are positive: 3x + 1 > 0 and x − 2 > 0, so x > 2.

questions.question: Simplify: 5x² − 2x + 3 − (2x² + 4x − 7)
questions.explanation: Distribute the negative: 5x² − 2x + 3 − 2x² − 4x + 7. Collect: x² terms: 3x². x terms: −2x − 4x = −6x. Constants: 3 + 7 = 10. Result: 3x² − 6x + 10. The +7 comes from −(−7) = +7 — flipping all signs inside the parentheses.
